event: Scenes from the Life of Saint Mary Magdalene
symbols: richly dressed figures — the worldly life of luxury that Magdalene will abandon for Christ
kneeling and listening postures — the conversion from worldly pursuits to spiritual devotion
pastoral landscape with distant buildings — the journey from the world to the Kingdom of God
gathering under trees — a teaching or preaching scene
description: A densely populated outdoor scene with numerous figures arranged in groups under trees in a landscape setting. At left, a woman in an elegant golden dress kneels. Around her, various figures in rich Renaissance clothing sit, stand, and converse — some in armor, some in elaborate dresses, some in simpler garb. A distant castle or city is visible in the background. The painting has the character of a continuous narrative, depicting multiple episodes from Magdalene's life in a single landscape. The palette is rich with reds, greens, golds, and earth tones.
theological_description: Lucas van Leyden's painting presents scenes from the life of Mary Magdalene, one of the most significant women in the Gospels. Magdalene's transformation from a life of sin to devoted discipleship embodies the radical power of Christ's forgiveness. Luke introduces her as 'a woman in that town who lived a sinful life' who wept at Jesus's feet, washing them with tears and anointing them with perfume (Luke 7:37-38). Jesus declared to her: 'Your sins are forgiven... Your faith has saved you; go in peace' (Luke 7:48-50). She became one of Jesus's most faithful followers, among the women who 'were helping to support them out of their own means' (Luke 8:2-3). The richly dressed figures in the painting suggest the worldly milieu from which Magdalene emerged. Most profoundly, she was the first witness of the Resurrection — Jesus appeared to her at the empty tomb and commissioned her: 'Go to my brothers and tell them' (John 20:17). The early Church called her 'the apostle to the apostles.' Her story embodies Jesus's teaching: 'I tell you, her many sins have been forgiven — as her great love has shown' (Luke 7:47).

scripture_refs.reason: The sinful woman anointing Jesus's feet — Magdalene's conversion and forgiveness
Mary Magdalene among the women who followed and supported Jesus's ministry
Magdalene as the first witness of the Resurrection — the climax of her life story
Great love flowing from great forgiveness — the theological heart of Magdalene's story

_meta.title: Scenes from the Life of St. Mary Magdalene
